# Monitoring and Statistics

**Files:** `cache/cache_entry_stats.h`, `cache/cache_entry_roles.h`, `cache/cache_entry_roles.cc`, `include/rocksdb/cache.h`, `include/rocksdb/block_cache_trace_writer.h`

## CacheEntryRole Tracking

Every cache entry is tagged with a `CacheEntryRole` (via `CacheItemHelper::role`). This enables per-role memory tracking without additional overhead on the hot path -- the role is stored in the helper struct that is already required for entry lifecycle management.

Role names are available in two formats:
- Camel case: `kCacheEntryRoleToCamelString` (e.g., "DataBlock", "FilterBlock")
- Hyphen-separated: `kCacheEntryRoleToHyphenString` (e.g., "data-block", "filter-block")

## Block Cache Entry Stats

`DB::Properties::kBlockCacheEntryStats` provides a map of per-role cache usage statistics. The map keys are generated by `BlockCacheEntryStatsMapKeys`:

| Key | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| `CacheId` | Unique identifier for this cache instance |
| `CacheCapacityBytes` | Maximum configured capacity |
| `LastCollectionDurationSeconds` | Time spent on the last stats collection scan |
| `LastCollectionAgeSeconds` | How long ago the last collection completed |
| `EntryCount(role)` | Number of entries for each `CacheEntryRole` |
| `UsedBytes(role)` | Total charge for entries of each role |
| `UsedPercent(role)` | Percentage of capacity used by each role |

## CacheEntryStatsCollector

`CacheEntryStatsCollector` (see `cache/cache_entry_stats.h`) is a generic framework for gathering per-entry stats by iterating over cache contents with `ApplyToAllEntries`.

Design choices to minimize performance impact:

- **One collector per cache**: the collector itself is stored as a cache entry, ensuring only one instance exists per cache regardless of how many column families or DBs share it
- **Time-based caching**: recent stats are reused within a caller-controlled window. The actual thresholds used by `InternalStats` are: background mode uses 180 seconds or 500x the last scan duration (whichever is longer); foreground mode uses 10 seconds or 10x
- **Mutex-protected collection**: only one thread can collect stats at a time; others get the most recent saved results

The scan iterates over all entries via `ApplyToAllEntries()`, which in `ShardedCache` processes one entry per lock acquisition per shard (the `average_entries_per_lock` parameter is clamped to 1) to minimize impact on concurrent cache operations.

## Cache Statistics (Tickers)

Key statistics tickers related to cache operations (see `include/rocksdb/statistics.h`):

| Ticker | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `BLOCK_CACHE_MISS` | Total block cache misses |
| `BLOCK_CACHE_HIT` | Total block cache hits |
| `BLOCK_CACHE_ADD` | New blocks added to cache |
| `BLOCK_CACHE_ADD_FAILURES` | Failed cache insertions (strict_capacity_limit) |
| `BLOCK_CACHE_DATA_MISS` / `_HIT` / `_ADD` | Data block specific |
| `BLOCK_CACHE_INDEX_MISS` / `_HIT` / `_ADD` | Index block specific |
| `BLOCK_CACHE_FILTER_MISS` / `_HIT` / `_ADD` | Filter block specific |
| `BLOCK_CACHE_BYTES_READ` | Bytes read from cache |
| `BLOCK_CACHE_BYTES_WRITE` | Bytes written to cache |
| `SECONDARY_CACHE_HITS` | Total secondary cache hits |
| `SECONDARY_CACHE_FILTER_HITS` | Filter hits from secondary cache |
| `SECONDARY_CACHE_INDEX_HITS` | Index hits from secondary cache |
| `SECONDARY_CACHE_DATA_HITS` | Data hits from secondary cache |
| `COMPRESSED_SECONDARY_CACHE_DUMMY_HITS` | Dummy entry hits in compressed secondary cache |
| `COMPRESSED_SECONDARY_CACHE_HITS` | Real entry hits in compressed secondary cache |
| `COMPRESSED_SECONDARY_CACHE_PROMOTIONS` | Entries promoted from compressed secondary cache |
| `COMPRESSED_SECONDARY_CACHE_PROMOTION_SKIPS` | Promotion skips (entry not worth promoting) |

## Perf Context Counters

Per-request counters available via `get_perf_context()`:

| Counter | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `block_cache_hit_count` | Hits in current request |
| `block_cache_miss_count` | Misses in current request |
| `secondary_cache_hit_count` | Secondary cache hits |
| `block_cache_standalone_handle_count` | Standalone handles created (secondary cache promotion) |
| `block_cache_real_handle_count` | Regular handles created (secondary cache promotion) |

## Block Cache Tracing

RocksDB supports detailed block cache access tracing for offline analysis. The tracing infrastructure (see `include/rocksdb/block_cache_trace_writer.h`) records cache accesses with metadata including:
- Block type, caller, key, data size
- Whether the access was a hit or miss
- Whether referenced data was already in cache
- Block offset and SST file information

The `sampling_frequency` option in `BlockCacheTraceOptions` controls how many requests are captured (default 1 = every request; higher values sample 1 in N).

Traces can be analyzed using the `block_cache_trace_analyzer` tool, and used with the simulation cache (see below) to evaluate alternative cache configurations.

## Simulation Cache

`SimCache` (see `include/rocksdb/utilities/sim_cache.h`) wraps a real cache with a simulated cache of a different size. It counts simulated hits and misses to predict the effect of a different cache size without actually changing the running configuration. This is useful for capacity planning.

## Cache Reporting

`Cache::ReportProblems()` is a periodic diagnostic that implementations can override to log warnings or errors. HyperClockCache uses this to report:
- Load factor issues (table too full)
- Excessive eviction effort (thrashing)
- Potential performance problems

This is intended to be called periodically by background threads and does not need to be efficient.
